title: Deputy Managing Director, Group R&D
description:
Job Description

The Deputy Managing Director, Group R&D reports to the Managing Director and is responsible for leading group‑wide research and development activities. The role focuses on optimizing the research portfolio and accelerating value creation at the Singapore R&D hub, maximizing technological competitiveness and business contribution in alignment with corporate strategy.

The incumbent is also responsible for maximizing value while minimizing risks related to human resources, equipment, and intellectual property, and ensuring safety, quality, and regulatory compliance.

Duties and Responsibilities

R&D Strategy Development and Execution

  • Formulate research roadmaps aligned with the company’s mid‑ to long‑term strategies.
  • Integrate core technologies (polymer materials, processing technologies, etc.) with applications (fibers, packaging materials, etc.) to accelerate new business creation.

Value Creation

  • Manage the process from mass production transfer to customer implementation.
  • Ensure speed and quality in joint research, venture collaboration, proof‑of‑concept activities, and customer evaluations.

Talent and Organizational Development

  • Train and mentor team members, including succession planning.
  • Maximize organizational performance and output.

Safety, Quality, and Compliance

  • Promote a strong safety culture and continuous improvement in accordance with local regulations and internal policies.
  • Manage research ethics and information security.

Budgeting and Resource Allocation

  • Optimize allocation and execution of personnel and investment budgets with clear visibility of cost and value.

Stakeholder and External Relations

  • Build and maintain relationships with corporate headquarters, customers, academic institutions, and external organizations.

Job Requirements

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Chemistry, Polymer Materials, Fiber Engineering, or related fields.

Experience

  • Over 20 years of experience in resin processing, R&D, scale‑up, or commercialization, including more than 10 years in management roles.
  • Experience in production engineering of synthetic fibers, including melt spinning of thermoplastic resins.
  • Experience in joint research and technology acquisition with customers or external organizations.
  • Experience in R&D portfolio management, strategy development, budget management, and KPI design.

Skills and Competencies

  • Strong cross‑cultural communication, negotiation, and talent management skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast‑paced environment and manage high‑pressure situations.
  • Basic proficiency in Japanese is advantageous for communication with Japan headquarters and customers.

Additional Information

  • Willingness to travel as required for customer engagement and industry‑related activities.
